note: seq=59 (ASYNC LANE RAMP on Hetzner, PG-safe, detached). Kicks /opt/atlas/async_ramp.py DETACHED (start_new_session) so the ~12-min staggered ramp never blocks the 90s puller. It steps ATLAS_ASYNC_LANES 32->128->256->512 (rewriting the unit's inline env + daemon-reload + restart atlas-enrich-async@1, pool 8->10->12), holds 180s to stabilize, and measures combined done-rate + bounded async PG conns (application_name=atlas_enrich_async) + RSS at each step, publishing status/hetzner/async-ramp.json cumulatively. PG-safe guard: stops if pg_total>=90. Live worker fleet untouched. The detached process inherits STATUS_TOKEN for publishing. Allowlist-safe (/opt/atlas/).

steps.note: seq=59: detached async ramp 32->128->256->512, measure each, publish async-ramp.json.

steps.cmd: /opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import hashlib,sys; w={'/opt/atlas/async_ramp.py':'518d9da3ebc705469025a6cdcf79f5f2c403236a91a81ad08f0809f575f09ab3'}; [sys.exit('SHA MISMATCH '+p) for p,h in w.items() if hashlib.sha256(open(p,'rb').read()).hexdigest()!=h]; print('sha ok')"

/opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import subprocess,os; subprocess.Popen(['/opt/atlas/venv/bin/python','/opt/atlas/async_ramp.py'], env=dict(os.environ), start_new_session=True); print('async ramp kicked (detached)')"
